notes: Mid-sentence juga between subject and verb ('dia juga datang' = 'they also came'). English rephrases to 'They came too' to match the rule equivalent ' too' appended at sentence end (rather than 'They also came' which would be more natural English but break idempotency). LIMITATION ACKNOWLEDGED: when NMT preserves the marker, the substitution happens at the marker's position; when NMT drops it, the inject is sentence-final. This is documented in the rule's design notes. VERIFY WITH GIRLFRIEND: 'They came too' is the safer rendering for this rule; 'They also came' would be more natural but mid-sentence inject is harder.
